examples.explanation: This pseudocode demonstrates RSA key generation, computing both the public key (n, e) and private key (d). It uses extended Euclidean algorithm to compute the modular inverse.
examples.title: RSA Key Generation Algorithm
examples.code:
BEGIN EXTENDED_GCD(a, b)
  // Recursive extended Euclidean algorithm
  IF b = 0 THEN
    RETURN (a, 1, 0)  // gcd(a,b) = a, coefficients: 1, 0
  END IF
  
  (g, x1, y1) ← EXTENDED_GCD(b, a mod b)
  
  // Update coefficients
  x ← y1
  y ← x1 - (a div b) × y1
  
  RETURN (g, x, y)
END

examples.explanation: This code implements RSA encryption and decryption using modular exponentiation with the square-and-multiply algorithm for efficiency. Encryption: C = M^e mod n; Decryption: M = C^d mod n.
examples.timeComplexity: O(log e) = O(k) for k-bit exponent
examples.title: RSA Encryption and Decryption
examples.code:
BEGIN RSA_ENCRYPT(message M, public_key (e, n))
  // Input: M must be less than n
  // Output: ciphertext C = M^e mod n
  
  IF M ≥ n THEN
    RETURN ERROR: Message too large for modulus
  END IF
  
  C ← MODULAR_EXPONENTIATION(M, e, n)
  RETURN C
END

BEGIN RSA_DECRYPT(ciphertext C, private_key (d, n))
  // Output: message M = C^d mod n
  
  M ← MODULAR_EXPONENTIATION(C, d, n)
  RETURN M
END

BEGIN MODULAR_EXPONENTIATION(base, exp, mod)
  // Square-and-multiply (binary exponentiation)
  // Efficient: O(log exp) modular multiplications
  
  result ← 1
  base ← base mod mod
  
  WHILE exp > 0 DO
    // If current bit of exponent is 1, multiply result by base
    IF (exp mod 2) = 1 THEN
      result ← (result × base) mod mod
    END IF
    
    // Square the base
    base ← (base × base) mod mod
    
    // Right shift exponent (divide by 2)
    exp ← exp div 2
  END WHILE
  
  RETURN result
END

examples.explanation: This implementation shows CRT-based RSA decryption optimization, which provides approximately 4× speedup by computing modular exponentiation modulo p and q separately (smaller moduli), then combining the results.
examples.title: RSA Decryption using CRT Optimization
